items.extraction_note: Wounded commanders and replacements change the emotional and organizational memory of a vehicle or unit.

source: The Battle of Carentan (8-15 June): Utah Beach to Cherbourg
items.extraction_note: Orders not received and chaotic terrain create hesitation and local pause.
Stress and exhaustion shape endurance, attention, and response speed.

items.behavior: survivor memory of enemy and friendly losses
items.extraction_note: Survivor accounts and friendly-fire incidents show memory as a tactical artifact.
